description: Use this API to add OPF bus attribute data for all records in a specified subsystem of the working case. This API routine was first introduced in release 28.0.0. ierr = opf_bus_subsys(sid, all, intgar, realar, useval)
python_syntax: ierr = opf_bus_subsys(sid, all, intgar, realar, useval)

parameters.name: sid
parameters.description: A valid subsystem id from 0 to 11. a subsystem must have been previously defined. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: all
parameters.description: All buses or specified subsystem flag. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: useval
parameters.description: An array of five elements specifying (default treatment same as value of 0). (input).

all.description: process all buses, use existing data values.
process all buses, use default data values.

intgar.description: limit type imposed on the bus voltage limits (default is the current value; initialized to 1).
indicates what to do with the defined voltage limits (default treatment same as value of 0).
leave voltage limits as defined by REALAR(1) and REALAR(2).
fix normal voltage limit at present voltage values, ignoring any values defined by REALAR(1) and REALAR(2).
allow normal voltage limits to open up if they exceed the bounds defined by REALAR(1) or REALAR(2).
set normal and emergency OPF voltage limits to the normal and emergency voltage limits as defined in the network bus data.
indicates which bus types should be processed (default treatment same as value of 0).
only Type 2 and 3 buses in subsystem.

realar.description: normal maximum voltage limit in pu (default is the current value; initialized to 9999.0).
normal minimum voltage limit in pu (default is the current value; initialized to -9999.0).
emergency maximum voltage limit in pu (default is the current value; initialized to 9999.0).
emergency minimum voltage limit in pu (default is the current value; initialized to -9999.0).
soft-limit penalty weight (default is the current value; initialized to 1.0).

useval.description: indicates how the value of REALAR(1) should be used.
if the value of REALAR(1) is to be used as defined.
if the value of REALAR(1) is to be multiplied by the present value of the normal bus voltage minimum limit.
if the value of REALAR(1) is to be added to the present value of the normal bus voltage minimum limit.
indicates how the value of REALAR(2) should be used.
if the value of REALAR(2) is to be used as defined.
if the value of REALAR(2) is to be multiplied by the present value of the normal bus voltage maximum limit.
if the value of REALAR(2) is to be added to the present value of the normal bus voltage maximum limit.
indicates how the value of REALAR(3) should be used.
if the value of REALAR(3) is to be used as defined.
if the value of REALAR(3) is to be multiplied by the present value of the emergency bus voltage minimum limit.
if the value of REALAR(3) is to be added to the present value of the emergency bus voltage minimum limit.
indicates how the value of REALAR(4) should be used.
if the value of REALAR(4) is to be used as defined.
if the value of REALAR(4) is to be multiplied by the present value of the emergency bus voltage maximum limit.
if the value of REALAR(4) is to be added to the present value of the emergency bus voltage maximum limit.
indicates how the value of PENWGT should be used.
if the value of PENWGT is to be used as defined.
if the value of PENWGT is to be multiplied by the present value of the soft limit penalty weight.
if the value of PENWGT is to be added to the present value of the soft limit penalty weight.

error_codes.description: invalid SID value or subsystem SID is not defined.
prerequisite requirements for API are not met.

non-fatal error occurred. Warning messages are as follows: - Invalid limit type specified. - Maximum normal voltage limit less than minimum limit. - Maximum emergency voltage limit less than minimum limit.
